questions.question: What is this project about?
questions.reference_answer: It is a production-style RAG pipeline that supports many file types, retrieval, generation, evaluation, and benchmarking.

questions.question: What does the pipeline do?
questions.reference_answer: It ingests files, normalizes content, chunks documents, retrieves context, generates answers, evaluates them, and saves reports.

questions.question: Which metrics are tracked?
questions.reference_answer: The project tracks context precision, context recall, faithfulness, answer relevancy, and reference similarity in benchmark mode.

questions.question: How does the system handle different file types?
questions.reference_answer: It uses format-aware ingestion and normalization so PDFs, DOCX, HTML, CSV, XLSX, markdown, and code-like files can all flow into the same RAG pipeline.

questions.question: What makes the retrieval layer production-oriented?
questions.reference_answer: It combines BM25, dense retrieval, and reciprocal rank fusion, with a retriever orchestrator and prompt-based generation.

questions.question: Why do we save benchmark reports?
questions.reference_answer: We save benchmark reports so we can compare runs over time, inspect per-question results, and track improvement.

questions.question: What are the main pieces of the system?
questions.reference_answer: The main pieces are ingestion, chunking, retrieval orchestration, generation, evaluation, reporting, and benchmark comparison.

questions.question: How is the pipeline made measurable?
questions.reference_answer: The pipeline is measurable because it saves evaluation reports, benchmark summaries, and comparison reports for each run.
